}\n\n    // Edge case tests for indentation parsing\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_very_long_indentation() {\n        // Very long indentation (100+ spaces)\n        let long_indent = \" \".repeat(100) + \"key: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(&long_indent), 100);\n\n        let very_long_indent = \" \".repeat(200) + \"key: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(&very_long_indent), 200);\n\n        let extremely_long_indent = \" \".repeat(500) + \"key: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(&extremely_long_indent), 500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_hash_only() {\n        // Lines with only \"#\" symbol (no text after)\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    #\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_whitespace_and_hash() {\n        // Lines with only whitespace and \"#\"\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"   #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_unicode_content() {\n        // Unicode content with indentation\n        let unicode_line = \"  clé: valeur\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line), 2);\n\n        let unicode_line2 = \"    名前: 値\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line2), 4);\n\n        let unicode_line3 = \"\\t用户名: 密码\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line3), 1);\n\n        let unicode_line4 = \"      ελληνικά: Greek\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line4), 6);\n\n        let unicode_line5 = \"    🎨: emoji\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line5), 4);\n\n        let unicode_line6 = \"  ñoño: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line6), 2);\n\n        let unicode_line7 = \"    -key-with-accents: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line7), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_mixed_whitespace() {\n        // Mixed whitespace in comment lines\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t# comment\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t# comment\"), 3);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t # comment\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t # comment\"), 3);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t  # comment\"), 5);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t \\t# comment\"), 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_very_long_indentation() {\n        // Very long indentation (100+ spaces)\n        let long_indent = \" \".repeat(100) + \"key: value\";\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(&long_indent);\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 100);\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 0);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 100);\n        assert!(info.is_spaces_only());\n        assert!(!info.is_mixed());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_mixed_whitespace_variations() {\n        // Various mixed whitespace patterns\n        let info1 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\" \\t#\");\n        assert_eq!(info1.leading_spaces, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info1.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info1.is_mixed());\n\n        let info2 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"  \\t#\");\n        assert_eq!(info2.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info2.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info2.is_mixed());\n\n        let info3 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t #\");\n        assert_eq!(info3.leading_spaces, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info3.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info3.is_mixed());\n\n        let info4 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t\\t  #\");\n        assert_eq!(info4.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info4.leading_tabs, 2);\n        assert!(info4.is_mixed());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_unicode_with_indentation() {\n        // Unicode content classification with various indentation levels\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  clé: valeur\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    名前: 値\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t用户名: 密码\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  # комментарий\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    # תגובה\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_mixed_whitespace_with_content() {\n        // Mixed whitespace with different content types\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t key: value\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t # comment\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t - item\"), LineType::SequenceItem);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t  \"), LineType::Blank);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_empty_lines_edge_cases() {\n        // Empty and whitespace-only edge cases\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t\\t\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t \"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t \"), 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_only_comments_edge_cases() {\n        // Comments with various indentation patterns\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"#\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" #\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  #\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t#\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t#\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t #\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"    # comment\"), 4);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t # deep comment\"), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_unicode_edge_cases() {\n        // Unicode with various edge cases\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"  👍: thumbs up\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 2);\n\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"    🔥: fire\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 4);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 4);\n\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t🌍: world\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_combined_edge_cases() {\n        // Combined edge case testing\n        // Very long indentation with various content types\n        let very_long_indent = \" \".repeat(100);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}key: value\", very_long_indent)), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}# comment\", very_long_indent)), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}- item\", very_long_indent)), LineType::SequenceItem);\n\n        // Hash only with various indentation\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}#\", \" \".repeat(50))), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}#\", \"\\t\".repeat(10))), LineType::Comment);\n\n        // Mixed whitespace with unicode\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t clé: valeur\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t 用户名: 密码\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t \\t # 注释\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    // Integration tests for comment filtering in key detection\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_skips_full_line_comments() {\n        // Full-line comments should be ignored\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"# This is a comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Full-line comment should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  # Indented comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Indented full-line comment should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"\\t# Tab-indented comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Tab-indented comment should not be detected as key\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_strips_inline_comments() {\n        // Inline comments should be stripped before processing\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: value # inline comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with inline comment should still be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"key\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  nested: value # comment here\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Nested key with inline comment should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"nested\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_preserves_hashes_in_values() {\n        // Hashes in URLs and values should be preserved\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"url: http://example.com#anchor\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with URL hash should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"url\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"http://example.com#anchor\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"link: https://api.example.com/v1#section # comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with URL hash and inline comment should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"link\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"https://api.example.com/v1#section\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"text: value # hash # in # value\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with hash characters in value should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        // The first # preceded by whitespace starts the comment, so value should be \"value\"\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_preserves_quoted_hashes() {\n        // Hashes in quoted strings should be preserved\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: \\\"value with # hash\\\"\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with quoted hash should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n It returns a list of validation errors\n107\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n108\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n109\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n110\t\n111\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n112\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n113\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n114\t\n115\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n116\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n117\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n118\t                {\n119\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n120\t                }\n121\t                scope_stack.reset();\n122\t                continue;\n123\t            }\n124\t\n125\t            // Skip YAML directives\n126\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n127\t                continue;\n128\t            }\n129\t\n130\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n131\t\n132\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n133\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n134\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n135\t            if indent_changed {\n136\t                // Check if this line has a key context\n137\t                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n138\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n139\t            }\n140\t\n141\t            // Handle blank lines and comments\n142\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n143\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n144\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n145\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n146\t                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n147\t                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n148\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n149\t                }\n150\t                continue;\n151\t            }\n152\t\n153\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n154\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n155\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n156\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n157\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n158\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n159\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n160\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n161\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n162\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n163\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n164\t                                    dup_err.message()\n165\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n166\t                            }\n167\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n168\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n169\t                            {\n170\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n171\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n172\t                            }\n173\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n174\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n175\t                                line_num_1index,\n176\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n177\t                            );\n178\t                        } else {\n179\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n180\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n181\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n182\t                            {\n183\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n184\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n185\t                            }\n186\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n187\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n188\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n189\t                                    dup_err.message()\n190\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n191\t                            }\n192\t                        }\n193\t                    } else {\n194\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n195\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n196\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n197\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n198\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n199\t                        {\n200\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n201\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n202\t                        }\n203\t                    }\n204\t                }\n205\t                Ordering::Less => {\n206\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n207\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n208\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n209\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n210\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n211\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n212\t\n213\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n214\t                    {\n215\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n216\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n217\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n218\t                    }\n219\t\n220\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n221\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n222\t                    }\n223\t\n224\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n225\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n226\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n227\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n228\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n229\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n230\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n231\t                                    dup_err.message()\n232\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n233\t                            }\n234\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n235\t                            {\n236\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n237\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n238\t                            }\n239\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n240\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n241\t                                line_num_1index,\n242\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n243\t                            );\n244\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n245\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n246\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n247\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n248\t                                    dup_err.message()\n249\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n250\t                            }\n251\t                        }\n252\t                    }\n253\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n254\t                }\n255\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n256\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n257\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n258\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n259\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n260\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n261\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n262\t                            {\n263\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n264\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n265\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n266\t                            }\n267\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n268\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n269\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n270\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n271\t                                    dup_err.message()\n272\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n273\t                            }\n274\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n275\t                            {\n276\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n277\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n278\t                            }\n279\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n280\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n281\t                                line_num_1index,\n282\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n283\t                            );\n284\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n285\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n286\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n287\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n288\t                                    dup_err.message()\n289\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n290\t                            }\n291\t                        }\n292\t                    }\n293\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n294\t                }\n295\t            }\n296\t\n297\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n298\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n299\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n300\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n301\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n302\t                    {\n303\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n304\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n305\t                    }\n306\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n307\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n308\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n309\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n310\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n311\t                            dup_err.message()\n312\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n313\t                    }\n314\t                } else {\n315\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n316\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n317\t                    {\n318\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n319\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n320\t                    }\n321\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n322\t                }\n323\t            }\n324\t Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n            if indent_changed {\n                // Check if this line has a key context\n                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n            }\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n                    }\n\n                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                    }\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes,        if indent_changed {\n                // Check if this line has a key context\n                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n            }\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260714041819642e94dc0cf041f4","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_ab47d67cc3d448a09a0f7c04","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-4vdifj.md","content":"# YAML Scope Tracking Mechanism Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nThe NEEDLE YAML parser implements a sophisticated scope tracking system to accurately detect duplicate keys within their proper hierarchical contexts. This documentation explains the current implementation, how scope transitions are detected, and where indent-only detection should be added.\n\n## Core Data Structures\n\n### `Scope` - Single Mapping Context\n\nLocated in: `src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs`\n\n```rust\npub struct Scope {\n    pub indent_level: usize,           // Indentation level (spaces)\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,         // Keys defined within this scope\n    pub start_line: usize,             // Line where scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,    // Key that created this scope\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,           // Whether in {key: value} flow style\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,     // Whether within sequence\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>, // Unique ID for sequence items\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose**: Represents a single mapping context at a specific nesting level. Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of parent or sibling scopes.\n\n**Key Operations**:\n- `add_key()` - Adds a key, returns `true` if duplicate\n- `contains_key()` - Checks if key exists in this scope\n\n### `ScopeStack` - Hierarchical Scope Management\n\n```rust\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,                // Stack of active scopes (top = current)\n    base_indent: usize,               // Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,      // For generating unique sequence IDs\n    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>, // History of indent transitions\n    last_indent: usize,               // Last recorded indent level\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose**: Manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing, maintaining a stack where the top element is the current scope.\n\n**Key Methods**:\n- `current_scope()` - Get mutable reference to current scope\n- `enter_scope()` - Enter nested scope (indent increase)\n- `exit_to_scope()` - Exit to parent scope (indent decrease)\n- `add_key()` - Add key to current scope with duplicate detection\n- `get_scope_path()` - Get dot-separated path (e.g., \"services.web.database\")\n- `record_indent_transition()` - Track all indent changes\n- `process_indent_transition_without_key()` - Handle indent changes without key tokens\n\n### `KeyContext` - Key Token Classification\n\n```rust\npub enum KeyContext {\n    InlineScalar { key: String, value: String },  // \"key: value\"\n    ParentMapping { key: String },                // \"key:\" (creates new scope)\n    ParentSequence { key: String },               // \"key:\\n  - item\"\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose**: Classifies keys based on whether they create new scopes (parent keys) or have inline values.\n\n**Key Methods**:\n- `is_parent_key()` - Returns true if key creates a new scope\n- `is_inline_scalar()` - Returns true if key has inline value\n\n### `IndentTransition` - Indent Change Tracking\n\n```rust\npub struct IndentTransition {\n    pub line_number: usize,       // Where transition occurred\n    pub from_indent: usize,       // Previous indent level\n    pub to_indent: usize,         // New indent level\n    pub has_key: bool,           // Whether line had a key token\n    pub raw_line: String,         // Raw line content (for debugging)\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose**: Records all indentation changes during parsing, whether or not they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables analysis of indent transitions on blank lines, comments, and other non-key lines.\n\n**Key Methods**:\n- `is_increase()` - Check if indent increased\n- `is_decrease()` - Check if indent decreased\n- `is_without_key()` - Check if transition occurred without a key token\n\n## Current Scope Transition Detection\n\n### Detection Trigger: Line-by-Line Processing\n\nScope transitions are detected during line-by-line parsing in `src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs`:\n\n```rust\nfor (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n    let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n    \n    // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes\n    let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n    if indent_changed {\n        let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n        scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n    }\n    \n    // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n    match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n        Ordering::Greater => { /* Enter scope */ }\n        Ordering::Less => { /* Exit scope */ }\n        Ordering::Equal => { /* Same scope */ }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Three Transition Scenarios\n\n#### 1. Indent Increase → Enter Scope\n\n**Current Logic** (lines 388-436 in parser.rs):\n\n```rust\nOrdering::Greater => {\n    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n            // Add parent key to current scope\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n            \n            // Enter new scope for nested content\n            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                line_num_1index,\n                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n            );\n        } else {\n            // Inline scalar at deeper indent - no scope entry\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No key context - skip scope entry\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Dependency**: **Key tokens are REQUIRED**. Indent increases without a parent key do NOT trigger scope entry.\n\n**Example**:\n```yaml\nparent:          # Line 1: Parent key → enters scope\n  child: value   # Line 2: Indent increase + parent key → enters scope\n```\n\n#### 2. Indent Decrease → Exit Scope\n\n**Current Logic** (lines 437-479 in parser.rs):\n\n```rust\nOrdering::Less => {\n    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n    \n    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n            // This is a new parent key at this level\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                line_num_1index,\n                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Dependency**: **Key tokens are OPTIONAL for exit**. Scope exit occurs on indent decrease regardless of key presence.\n\n**Example**:\n```yaml\nparent:          # Line 1: Parent key\n  child: value   # Line 2: Nested\nsibling:         # Line 3: Indent decrease → exits scope\n```\n\n#### 3. Same Indent → Check for Sibling Keys\n\n**Current Logic** (lines 480-500 in parser.rs):\n\n```rust\nOrdering::Equal => {\n    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n            // Sibling parent key - exit and re-enter\n            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                line_num_1index,\n                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n            );\n        } else {\n            // Inline scalar - just add to current scope\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Dependency**: **Key tokens are REQUIRED**. Same indent with no key is a no-op.\n\n## Role of Key Tokens in Scope Changes\n\n### Critical Role\n\n**Key tokens are the PRIMARY TRIGGER for scope entry**. The parser uses `extract_key_context()` to determine if a line contains a mapping key, and only enters a new scope when:\n\n1. Indent increases AND\n2. Line contains a parent mapping key (`ParentMapping` variant)\n\n### Key Context Extraction\n\nLocated in: `src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs` (lines 1045-1118)\n\n```rust\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n    \n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n    \n    // Skip if key is empty or contains invalid characters\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() { return None; }\n    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n        return None;\n    }\n    \n    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\n    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\"- \") {\n        key[2..].trim()\n    } else {\n        key\n    };\n    \n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: actual_key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n    \n    Some(context)\n}\n```\n\n**What It Detects**:\n- Mapping keys with colons (`:`)\n- Distinguishes parent mappings (no value after colon) from inline scalars\n- Strips sequence dash prefix (`- `)\n- Rejects flow style mappings (contains `{`, `[`, etc.)\n\n**What It Ignores**:\n- Comment lines (starts with `#`)\n- Document markers (`---`, `...`)\n- Directives (`%YAML`)\n- Anchors (`&`), aliases (`*`), tags (`!`)\n- Sequence items without keys (lone `-`)\n\n### Key Token Limitations\n\n**Blank lines are ignored** (lines 377-383 in parser.rs):\n\n```rust\nif trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n    // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n    if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n        scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n    }\n    continue;\n}\n```\n\n**Current Behavior**:\n- Blank lines DO trigger `record_indent_transition()` for tracking\n- Blank lines DO call `process_indent_transition_without_key()` for exit\n- **Blank lines DO NOT trigger scope entry** even if indent increases\n\n**Comment lines are fully skipped**:\n- No indent transition processing\n- No scope changes of any kind\n\n## Current Indent Handling Logic\n\n### Indent Calculation\n\nLocated in: `src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs` (lines 732-763)\n\n```rust\npub fn calculate_indentation(line: &str) -> usize {\n    IndentationInfo::from_line(line).total_level\n}\n\npub struct IndentationInfo {\n    pub leading_spaces: usize,\n    pub leading_tabs: usize,\n    pub total_level: usize,\n}\n```\n\n**Behavior**:\n- Counts all leading whitespace characters (spaces + tabs)\n- Does NOT distinguish between spaces and tabs for basic tracking\n- Returns total count as indent level\n\n### Indent Transition Recording\n\nLocated in: `src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs` (lines 707-759)\n\n```rust\npub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n    // Only record if the indent actually changed\n    if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n        let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n            line_number,\n            self.last_indent,\n            new_indent,\n            has_key,\n            raw_line,\n        );\n        self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n        self.last_indent = new_indent;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Purpose**: Records ALL indent changes during parsing, creating a complete history of indentation transitions whether or not they occur on lines with key tokens.\n\n**Key Points**:\n- Records changes on ANY line type (blank, comment, key, etc.)\n- Tracks whether each transition had a key token\n- Stores raw line content for debugging\n- Maintains `last_indent` state across the entire parse\n\n### Indent-Only Transition Processing\n\nLocated in: `src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs` (lines 828-894)\n\n```rust\npub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\n    let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n    \n    match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\n        Ordering::Greater => {\n            // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\n            // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\n            // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\n            false\n        }\n        Ordering::Less => {\n            // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n            // This is valid even without a key\n            self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\n            true\n        }\n        Ordering::Equal => {\n            // Same indent - no scope change needed\n            false\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Current Limitation**: **Only processes exits, not entries**. Indent increases without keys are logged but don't trigger scope entry.\n\n## Where Indent-Only Detection Should Be Added\n\n### Current Gap\n\nThe parser has the infrastructure to detect and process indent-only transitions (via `record_indent_transition()` and `process_indent_transition_without_key()`), but it only uses this for scope **exit**, not scope **entry**.\n\n### Implementation Location\n\n**File**: `src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs`\n**Function**: `parse_str()` or `detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope()`\n**Lines**: ~388-436 (indent increase handling)\n\n### Proposed Enhancement\n\nAdd logic to handle indent increases on blank lines:\n\n```rust\nOrdering::Greater => {\n    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n            // EXISTING: Parent key → enter scope\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                line_num_1index,\n                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n            );\n        } else {\n            // EXISTING: Inline scalar → no scope entry\n            scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n        }\n    } else {\n        // NEW: Indent increased but no key context\n        // Check if this is a blank line with significant indent increase\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            // Enter scope for blank line indent increase\n            // Next content line will populate this scope\n            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                indent,\n                line_num_1index,\n                None  // No parent key yet\n            );\n        } else {\n            // Non-key, non-blank line - skip scope entry\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Use Cases for Indent-Only Entry\n\n1. **Blank lines before nested content**:\n   ```yaml\n   parent:\n   \n     child: value   # Blank line + indent increase should enter scope\n   ```\n\n2. **Comment blocks before nested content**:\n   ```yaml\n   parent:\n     # Comment block\n   \n     child: value   # Comment indent + blank line should enter scope\n   ```\n\n3. **Multi-line nested structures**:\n   ```yaml\n   services:\n   \n   web:\n     host: localhost\n   \n   database:\n     host: db.example.com\n   ```\n\n### Testing Strategy\n\nTest files demonstrate the gap:\n- `test_blank_line_yaml.rs` - Tests blank line handling\n- `test_sequence_scope.rs` - Tests sequence scope detection\n- `examples/scope_key_tracking_demo.rs` - Demonstrates scope tracking\n\n**Test Cases to Add**:\n1. Blank line with indent increase → should enter scope\n2. Comment line with indent increase → should NOT enter scope (current behavior is correct)\n3. Blank line then indent decrease → should exit scope (already works)\n4. Multiple blank lines at varying indents → should track properly\n\n## Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                        YAML Parser                              │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                                                                  │\n│  ┌────────────────┐                                              │\n│  │ Line-by-Line   │                                              │\n│  │ Processing     │                                              │\n│  └───────┬────────┘                                              │\n│          │                                                       │\n│          ▼                                                       │\n│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │              Indent Change Detection                      │   │\n│  │  calculate_indentation() → compare with current_indent   │   │\n│  └───────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│          │                                                       │\n│          ▼                                                       │\n│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │              Key Context Extraction                       │   │\n│  │  extract_key_context() → Some(KeyContext) or None        │   │\n│  └───────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│          │                                                       │\n│          ▼                                                       │\n│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │           Indent Transition Recording                     │   │\n│  │  record_indent_transition(line, indent, has_key, raw)    │   │\n│  └───────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│          │     │   │\n│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │   │\n│  │  │ ScopeStack { scopes: Vec<Scope> }                   │ │   │\n│  │  │                                                       │ │   │\n│  │  │ enter_scope()    → push new Scope                   │ │   │\n│  │  │ exit_to_scope()  → pop to target indent             │ │   │\n│  │  │ add_key()        → add to current scope              │ │   │\n│  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │   │\n│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│                                                                  │\n│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │              Duplicate Key Detection                       │   │\n│  │  check if key exists in current_scope.keys                │   │\n│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│                                                                  │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## Flow Diagram: Scope Transitions\n\n```\n                    Line Processing\n                          │\n                          ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────┐\n              │  Calculate Indent   │\n              └──────────┬──────────┘\n                         │\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────┐\n              │  Indent Changed?     │\n              └──────────┬──────────┘\n                    │/│\n           ┌────────┴────────┐\n           │                 │\n          Yes               No\n           │                 │\n           ▼                 │\n┌─────────────────────┐     │\n│  Extract Key Context│     │\n└──────────┬──────────┘     │\n           │                │\n   ┌───────┴────────┐      │\n   │                │      │\nParent Key       No Key\n   │                │      │\n   ▼                ▼      │\n┌─────────┐   ┌────────┐  │\n│ Compare │   │ Record │  │\n│ Indents │   │ Only   │  │\n└────┬────┘   │ Trans  │  │\n     │        └───┬────┘  │\n     │            │       │\n     ▼            ▼       │\n  ┌─────────────────┐     │\n  │  Indent Change   │     │\n  └────────┬─────────┘     │\n           │\n     ┌─────┼─────┬─────┐\n     │     │     │     │\n  Increase Equal Decrease\n     │     │     │     │\n     ▼     │     ▼     │\n┌──────┐  │  ┌──────┐ │\n│Enter │  │  │Exit  │ │\n│Scope │  │  │Scope │ │\n└───┬──┘  │  └───┬──┘ │\n    │     │      │    │\n    └─────┴──────┴────┘\n           │\n           ▼\n   ┌─────────────┐\n   │ Add Key to  │\n   │Current Scope│\n   └──────┬──────┘\n          │\n          ▼\n   ┌─────────────┐\n   │ Check for   │\n   │ Duplicates  │\n   └──────┬──────┘\n          │\n          ▼\n   ┌─────────────┐\n   │ Next Line   │\n   └─────────────┘\n```\n\n## Summary\n\n### Current State\n\n1. **Scope transitions are key-token dependent** - Indent increases only trigger scope entry when accompanied by a parent mapping key\n2. **Indent decreases work independently** - Scope exit occurs on indent decrease regardless of key presence\n3. **Blank lines partially processed** - Blank lines trigger indent tracking and scope exit, but NOT scope entry\n4. **Comprehensive tracking infrastructure exists** - `record_indent_transition()` and `IndentTransition` provide full history\n\n### Key Token Dependencies\n\n- **Scope Entry**: REQUIRES parent key (`ParentMapping`)\n- **Scope Exit**: Does NOT require key\n- **Scope Re-entry (sibling)**: REQUIRES parent key\n- **Indent Tracking**: Does NOT require key (tracks all changes)\n\n### Where to Add Indent-Only Detection\n\n**Location**: `src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs`, lines ~388-436\n\n**Enhancement**: Add logic to `Ordering::Greater` branch to enter scopes on blank lines with significant indent increases, even without parent keys.\n\n**Impact**: Would enable proper scope tracking for structures with blank lines between parent keys and nested content, improving duplicate key detection accuracy.\n\n## Related Files\n\n- `src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs` - Core scope data structures and management\n- `src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs` - Main parsing logic with scope transitions\n- `src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs` - Line classification and indent calculation\n- `src/parsers/yaml/syntax_detector.rs` -
